source_feedback: Committed-HEAD maintainer review of the generic leverage-classified v2 outputs. This corpus is for evaluating trust, presentation, and leverage meaning against a real maintainer reading, not for repo-specific analyzer logic.

verdicts.engineer_note: The strongest architecture signal is the component-facing barrel inside the largest mixed subsystem. Fan-in, instability, and cycle position matter more than file size.
verdicts.expected_v2_behavior: Keep this as a lead architecture signal and explain it as a boundary hub inside a mixed cycle, not as generic large-file debt.

verdicts.engineer_note: Cycle clustering is the strongest repo-wide architecture metric when it highlights mixed ownership and boundary ambiguity.
verdicts.expected_v2_behavior: Keep the cycle prominent as an architecture signal, but frame it as a watchpoint with cut-candidate context rather than a mandatory refactor queue.

verdicts.engineer_note: This is the best contained refactor target because the repo already declares it as a guarded shell with extracted owners.
verdicts.expected_v2_behavior: Surface this as a strong local refactor target when dependency breadth overlaps declared extracted-owner boundaries.

verdicts.engineer_note: This is a credible contained refactor target, but it should rank behind TaskPanel.tsx because the ownership signal is weaker.
verdicts.expected_v2_behavior: Keep this visible in the local-refactor bucket without letting it outrank stronger contained surfaces backed by clearer ownership guardrails.

verdicts.engineer_note: The useful question is whether domain or lifecycle policy is leaking into transport glue, not whether central fan-in alone is bad.
verdicts.expected_v2_behavior: Treat guarded transport facades as boundary-discipline findings and emphasize glue-leakage risk over raw hotspot language.

verdicts.engineer_note: Composition roots naturally have broad fan-out. The value here is to keep shell ownership from regrowing, not to force major surgery.
verdicts.expected_v2_behavior: Surface broad composition roots as regrowth watchpoints unless other evidence shows they are the highest-leverage architecture change.

verdicts.engineer_note: This public lifecycle facade still has meaningful pressure, but it should read as careful secondary cleanup rather than the lead architecture problem.
verdicts.expected_v2_behavior: Keep extracted-owner lifecycle facades visible as secondary cleanup when they overlap several real signals without being the highest-leverage target.

verdicts.engineer_note: The missing exhaustiveness is real, but it belongs in targeted hardening because the lifecycle model already exists elsewhere.
verdicts.expected_v2_behavior: Keep narrow exhaustiveness findings visible as hardening notes and do not let them lead the architecture summary.

verdicts.engineer_note: The script is a real maintenance burden, but it should not compete directly with app/runtime architecture priorities.
verdicts.expected_v2_behavior: Separate script and tooling pressure into tooling debt so it stays visible without distorting the main architectural story.

verdicts.engineer_note: This detector still claims live helpers are stale, so it should not influence maintainer-facing prioritization.
verdicts.expected_v2_behavior: Keep this detector quarantined as experimental until same-file and TSX helper usage are reliable.
